Consider a synchronous generator connected to an infinite bus by two identical parallel transmission lines as shown in the <image>. The transient reactance 'x' of the generator is 0.1 pu, and the mechanical power input to it is constant at 1.0 pu. Due to some previous disturbance, the rotor angle (\(\delta\)) is undergoing an undamped oscillation, with the maximum value of \(\delta(t)\) equal to \(130^\circ\). One of the parallel lines trips due to relay maloperation at an instant when \(\delta(t) = 130^\circ\) as shown in the <image>. The maximum value of the per unit line reactance, x, such that the system does not lose synchronism subsequent to this tripping is ________
after_value: Consider a synchronous generator connected to an infinite bus by two identical parallel transmission lines as shown in the image. One of the parallel lines trips due to relay maloperation at an instant when \(\delta(t) = 130^\circ\) as shown in the image. The maximum value of the per unit line reactance, x, such that the system does not lose synchronism subsequent to this tripping is ________

A three-phase slip-ring induction motor, provided with a commutator winding, is shown in the <image>. The motor rotates in a clockwise direction when the rotor windings are closed.

If the rotor winding is open-circuited and the system is made to run at rotational speed \( f_r \) with the help of a prime mover in an anti-clockwise direction, then the frequency of voltage across slip rings is \( f_1 \) and the frequency of voltage across commutator brushes is \( f_2 \). The values of \( f_1 \) and \( f_2 \) respectively are ______.
after_value:
A three-phase slip-ring induction motor, provided with a commutator winding, is shown in the figure. The motor rotates in a clockwise direction when the rotor windings are closed.

If the rotor winding is open-circuited and the system is made to run at rotational speed \( f_r \) with the help of a prime mover in an anti-clockwise direction, then the frequency of voltage across slip rings is \( f_1 \) and the frequency of voltage across commutator brushes is \( f_2 \). The values of \( f_1 \) and \( f_2 \), respectively, are ______.

before_value: \( f + f_r \) and \( f \)

Consider for CMOS circuit shown in <image>, where the gate voltage \( V_G \) of the n-MOSFET is increased from zero, while the gate voltage of the p-MOSFET is kept constant at 3 V. Assume that, for both transistors, the magnitude of the threshold voltage is 1 V and the product of the trans-conductance parameter and the \( \left(\frac{W}{L}\right) \) ratio, i.e. the quantity \( \mu C_{ox} \left(\frac{W}{L}\right) \), is 1 mA · V\(^{-2}\).

For small increase in \( V_G \) beyond 1V, which of the following gives the correct description of the region of operation of each MOSFET
after_value:
Consider the CMOS circuit shown in the figure, where the gate voltage \(V_G\) of the n-MOSFET is increased from zero, while the gate voltage of the p-MOSFET is kept constant at 3 V. Assume that, for both transistors, the magnitude of the threshold voltage is 1 V and the product of the trans-conductance parameter and the \(\left(\frac{W}{L}\right)\) ratio, i.e. the quantity \(\mu C_{ox} \left(\frac{W}{L}\right)\), is 1 mA·V\(^{-2}\).

For a small increase in \(V_G\) beyond 1 V, which of the following gives the correct description of the region of operation of each MOSFET?

before_value: n-MOSFET is in saturation and p-MOSFET is in triode region
after_value: n-MOSFET is in saturation and p-MOSFET is in triode region
